title: Meta Ads Video Editor (AI UGC / Performance Creative)
description:
Hey, I’m Carter — founder of a fast-growing DTC brand scaling through Meta 
 & Instagram) ads.

We’re looking for a high-level Meta Ads Video Editor who understands how to turn simple scripts into scroll-stopping ads.

We focus on AI UGC-style creatives, so this role is more than just editing — you need to understand what actually makes ads convert.



Your Role:

Turn scripts into high-converting Meta ads using AI UGC.



Structure:



1 concept = 3 videos

Same body, different hooks

You will:

Create or source AI UGC-style footage

Edit clean, natural, iPhone-style ads (not overproduced)

Swap hooks while keeping the body consistent

Keep pacing fast and engaging



Workload (Important)

2 concepts (6 videos) every 2 days

Deadlines are strict and very important for scaling



System / Workflow

You’ll have access to a private dashboard with your own profile where you can:

View your tasks

See deadlines clearly

Access scripts and resources

Track your payroll dates

Everything is organized to keep work smooth and clear.





What We’re Looking For



Experience with Meta ads / DTC brands

Strong understanding of hooks (first 1–3 seconds matter most)

Knows how to keep edits simple, clean, and native

Understands retention and performance





Not a Fit If



You only edit YouTube content

You over-edit with effects/transitions

You don’t understand direct response ads



Bonus

Experience with AI tools (Higgsfield, Sora, etc.)

Can replicate winning ad styles





How to Apply

Send:

Examples of Meta ads you’ve edited (UGC or AI UGC preferred)

Your process



Confirm you understand:

1 concept = 3 videos

Same body, different hooks



If you’re good, this can turn into long-term consistent work.

title: Experienced Video Editor - Urgent
description:
You don't just edit videos. You join a fabulous team + You make people stop scrolling.



Take time to check this new opportunity that it might be for you!

Video Editor - Social Media (with possibility to work full-time)

$7-8/hr, 20/week Central US



We're growing our content production across multiple US clients and need an experienced editor who thinks in hooks, works independently, and can make a roofing company's social content feel as sharp as any top consumer brand. This starts at 20 hours a week with a clear path to full-time for the right person.



Join a US-based fractional CMO firm that serves trades businesses in HVAC, solar, roofing, plumbing, and masonry. We embed with our clients as their full marketing leadership team: strategy, execution, and everything in between. The content we produce represents real businesses built by hardworking owners. We take the quality of our work seriously.



What you'll do:

-Edit short-form videos for social media (Instagram Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, and
 etc.) across multiple US client accounts

-Work from raw footage and briefs, you own the process from first cut to final export

-Handle captions, motion graphics, sound design, and color grading

-Manage multiple client timelines simultaneously with zero hand-holding

-Proactively bring creative ideas that improve content performance



If you have these, we're looking exactly for you!

-6+ years editing social-first video content, of course your portfolio proves it!

-Fluency in Premiere Pro, Final Cut, or DaVinci Resolve or similar softwares

-Strong instinct for hook structure, pacing, and content that converts

-After Effects or motion graphics experience (strong plus)

-Excellent English communication on both written and spoken

-Proactive, self-managed work style you flag issues early and never need to be chased

-Availability during Central US business hours (CST/CDT)



Why Join Us?

-Work with a high-standards US marketing team that actually invests in its people

-Real client impact, your edits help real business owners grow

-Start part-time, prove your value, grow into a full-time role

-Fully remote, work from anywhere in the Philippines

-Fast-moving environment where great work gets recognized and rewarded



Feels like this is you?

Send your application to 
 with the subject line exactly as written:

Stop The Search For A Video Editor, [Your Full Name] is here!

Your
 must include:

1. Your resume - highlight AI tools, marketing platforms, and multi-client experience.

2. An updated 2-3 min Loom/Google video share your experience, tools you’ve used, and why you’re a great fit!

3. Portfolio/Work samples: Notion builds, social accounts, Canva designs, dashboards, websites, tools you've made.



Missing any of these? We won't be able to review it. Attention to detail is the job.

Applications reviewed on a rolling basis. Fast responders appreciated.



We're excited to meet you online!

title: VIDEO EDITOR — Part-Time 15–20h/week
description:
We are looking for an experienced and reliable Video Editor based in the Philippines to join our team and help us create high-converting direct-response video ads for a fast-growing international health and wellness e-commerce brand.

This is a long-term, part-time remote position with flexible scheduling and room to grow as the brand scales.



ABOUT THE BRAND

We are an international e-commerce brand that sells health and wellness products — specifically transdermal patches as an alternative to pills and supplements. Our customers are primarily based in the US, UK, and Europe. The brand is growing fast and we are looking for a creative video editor to grow with us.



WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

Edit direct-response video ads (primarily 9:16 vertical for Meta/Instagram Reels and TikTok)

Cut and pace footage to maximize viewer retention from the first frame

Work with AI-generated video clips, UGC-style content, and lifestyle footage

Add captions, sound effects, music, and motion graphics where needed

Implement strong hooks that stop the scroll within the first 3 seconds

Find suitable stock footage as B-Roll

Deliver polished ad creatives ready for paid social campaigns



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Must-Have:

Proven experience editing short-form video ads or social content

Strong understanding of direct-response video structure: hooks, pacing, CTA

Ability to empathize with the target audience (35–60 year olds) and edit accordingly

Portfolio of at least 3–5 reference videos you have edited

Your choice of editing software — use what you know best



Nice-to-Have:

Experience with AI content generation tools (Higgsfield, Kling, ElevenLabs, CapCut, or similar)

Familiarity with paid social ad creative formats and best practices

Experience editing for health, wellness, or supplement brands



SCHEDULE & HOURS

Part-time: approximately 15–20 hours per week

Flexible scheduling — we will find a rhythm that works for both sides

100% remote



WHAT WE OFFER

Competitive pay based on experience

Long-term position with a growing brand

Clear creative briefs provided for every project

Supportive and fast-moving team environment



HOW TO APPLY

If this sounds like a great fit for you, please include the following in your application:

A short introduction text about yourself and your video editing experience

3–5 reference videos you have edited — please share links (no downloads required)

Your preferred editing software and any AI tools you work with

Your expected hourly rate in USD



As a first step, shortlisted candidates will be asked to complete a paid test edit based on a provided brief. If the result is a good fit, we move forward together.



We review all applications carefully and will respond within 3–5 business days. 



Looking forward to hearing from you.

title: Thumbnail Designer  – YouTube Channel (300k subscribers)
description:
We are an animated documentary channel on YouTube with over 300,000 subscribers. We are looking for a skilled thumbnail artist who can create compelling, high-performing thumbnails using a hand-crafted design process starting from sketches and ending in polished final outputs.



This role is not for AI-only designers. We are specifically looking for artists who can think, sketch, and design intentionally, using AI only as a supporting tool, not as the primary method of creation.



What You’ll Do

 • Create thumbnails for each video using a two-step process:

 1. Hand-drawn sketch / concept layout

 2. Final polished thumbnail design

 • Translate video ideas into visually engaging, click-worthy concepts

 • Develop original compositions and visual storytelling elements

 • Refine sketches into high-quality thumbnails using design tools



 Core Requirement (VERY IMPORTANT)



This role requires a hand-crafted creative process.

 • You must be able to sketch ideas by hand (digitally or traditionally)

 • You must demonstrate original composition and concept development

 • AI tools may be used only as support, not as the primary method



Not acceptable:

 • Fully AI-generated thumbnails

 • Outputs that visibly look AI-generated

 • Reliance on prompt-only workflows



What we want:

 • Artist-driven thumbnails

 • Intentional design decisions

 • Work that feels crafted, not generated



What We’re Looking For



Artistic Skills (CRITICAL)

 • Strong drawing / sketching ability

 • Ability to quickly visualize and communicate ideas through sketches

 • Understanding of anatomy, shape, and exaggeration (especially animals)

 • Composition and layout design from scratch



 Concept & Creative Thinking

 • Ability to interpret a video idea and find the most clickable visual angle

 • Strong understanding of:

 • Emotion (danger, curiosity, shock)

 • Visual storytelling

 • Ability to create engaging hooks paired with visuals



Technical Skills

 • Adobe Photoshop (required)

 • Digital drawing tools (e.g., tablet workflows, brushes, sketch layers)

 • Image compositing and lighting effects

 • Typography for thumbnails (bold, readable text)



Workflow

 • Ability to:

 • Start with rough concept sketches

 • Iterate quickly

 • Deliver polished final thumbnails

 • Comfortable working within a repeatable template and style system



Deliverables Per Thumbnail

 • Initial Sketch

 • Rough layout of idea (composition + concept)

 • Final Thumbnail

 • Fully rendered, polished, YouTube-ready design



Important Note on AI



AI can be used for:

 • Reference gathering

 • Minor enhancements

 • Supporting ideation



However:



The core concept, composition, and final output must be artist-driven and manually executed.



Pay

 • $10 per thumbnail

 • $10 per compilation thumbnail

 • Ongoing weekly work for the right candidate



???? How to Apply

 1. Submit:

 • Examples of your work that include sketches + final outputs

 • Any relevant thumbnail or illustration work

 2. Be prepared to complete a short trial task



To confirm you’ve read this post, include the word “Burger” in your application. Applications without it will not be considered.



Applications that rely primarily on AI-generated artwork without demonstrating original sketching ability will not be considered.

title: Virtual Lead Generation Specialist – Car Finance Brokerage

You’ll own the digital outreach and qualification process for our car-finance brokerage. Using
, SMS, social-media ads, and paid search, you’ll generate, engage, and nurture prospects—handing off high-quality, finance-ready leads to our consultants and earning commission on every funded application.

Key Responsibilities & Tasks

Digital Campaigns & Outreach



Build and optimize lead-gen campaigns on Google Ads,
/Instagram, and LinkedIn.



Execute targeted
-drip sequences and SMS blasts to segmented audiences.

, and phone to assess eligibility.

Excellent written and verbal communication; strong copywriting skills.

Commission: Tiered bonus per funded finance application



PHP 1000 per approved deal from the 11th  deal onward in the same calendar month

title: Social Media Manager & Designer
description:
We're looking for a creative, trend-aware Social Media Manager & Designer to build and grow our yoga studio's online presence. You'll be responsible for the look, feel, and strategy of our social channels by creating content, directing on-site teams for footage, and making sure everything we post is polished and on-brand.



- What You'll Do

Manage and grow our presence across TikTok, Instagram, and other relevant platforms

Stay ahead of trends on TikTok & reels and identify opportunities to adapt them to our brand in a way that feels intentional, not forced

Build and maintain a content calendar.

Write captions, hooks, and copy that match our brand voice

Monitor performance and adjust strategy based on what's working

Invite influencers & think of marketing/ strategy ideas



- Content Direction & Design

Create graphics, reels covers, stories, and promotional assets with a clean, elevated aesthetic

Brief on-site assistants on exactly what footage or photos you need and review what they send before it goes anywhere

Edit and repurpose raw content into polished, publishable posts

Maintain visual consistency across all platforms and locations



- Marketing & Strategy

Bring proactive ideas for campaigns, launches, seasonal promotions, and collaborations

Identify growth opportunities like partnerships, trending sounds, community engagement

Support class and package promotions with timely, well-designed content



- What We're Looking For

A strong, demonstrable eye for aesthetic — elevated, cohesive, not generic

Deep familiarity with TikTok trends and how to apply them strategically

Proficiency in design tools (Canva, Adobe, CapCut, or similar)

Ability to direct others for content capture without being on-site yourself

Self-directed and proactive — you bring ideas, you don't wait to be told what to post

Experience managing social media for a lifestyle, wellness, or hospitality brand is a strong plus

title: TikTok Matrix Operations Specialist — Multi-Account Growth (Health/Wellness)
description:
About the Role:

We operate a TikTok account matrix system (20-200 accounts) for a US-based wellness brand in the peptide/biohacking space. We're looking for an experienced matrix operator who knows how to scale content production, manage account portfolios, and drive leads through volume + smart targeting — not viral lottery tickets.

This Role is NOT for:



Pure creative content creators

People who've only managed 1-3 accounts

Anyone who thinks "growth hacking" means following trends



This Role IS for:



Operators who've run 20+ accounts simultaneously

People who think in systems, templates, and data

Anyone who can produce 50+ videos in a day using batch editing workflows



What You'll Do:



Operate our matrix management system to run 50-100+ TikTok accounts

Build content templates that can be batch-produced into 100+ variants

Manage account warming, IP rotation, and platform compliance

Track per-account performance, kill dead accounts, scale winners

Build a content variation library (hooks, B-roll, music, captions)

Identify and replicate winning patterns across the network

Train and manage 1-2 junior editors as we scale



Required Experience:



2+ years running TikTok account matrices (please be specific in your application: how many accounts, what niche, what tools)

Hands-on experience with anti-detection browsers (AdsPower, BitBrowser, Multilogin, or similar)

Mastery of CapCut batch workflows OR similar batch editing pipelines

Understanding of TikTok's algorithm and shadowban patterns

Experience producing 100+ short videos per week

Strong English writing — you'll script and caption in US English



Highly Preferred:



Experience in regulated niches (supplements, CBD, weight loss, adult, gambling)

Understanding of Chinese matrix tools (??, ???, ???, etc.) — many of our reference workflows come from Chinese operators

Lead qualification experience (TikTok DM ? WhatsApp ? sales handoff)



Compensation Structure:



Base: $
/month (based on track record)

Per-account performance bonus: $5-15/month per account hitting growth targets

Lead generation bonus: $3-8 per qualified lead

Realistic total comp: $
/month for top performers



Application Requirements (be specific or don't apply):



List the largest matrix you've personally operated (# of accounts, niche, results)

Screenshot of your current/recent matrix dashboard (blur sensitive info)

Which anti-detect browser do you use and why?

What's your daily video production capacity using batch workflows?

Have you ever operated in restricted niches? Which ones?

title: Amazon Product Research & Sourcing Expert
description:
Olvy is a fast-growing Dutch e-commerce brand selling premium home appliances across Amazon EU, 
, Kaufland and other marketplaces. We are expanding aggressively and are building a team that operates independently and at scale. We move fast, work structured, and hold high standards across everything we do.



We are looking for a sharp, detail-oriented Product Sourcer who will own our product research and supplier pipeline. You will take input from our CEO (category, target specs, target price) and independently source the best options from Alibaba and other platforms. You will also track and follow up on all potential products from trade fairs such as Canton Fair, compare suppliers, and deliver clear overviews so we can make fast, informed decisions.



This is a key role in our growth. We are launching new products every quarter and we need someone who can keep up with that pace and bring structure to the process.



What You Will Do



• Proven experience in product sourcing or procurement, preferably in consumer goods or e-commerce

• Strong eye for product design and what makes a product stand out in a competitive market

• Excellent command of Alibaba, 1688, and similar platforms

• Highly structured and organized, able to manage multiple product pipelines simultaneously

• Strong communicator in English, both written and spoken

• Proactive, self-directed and able to work without daily guidance

• Experience working with European or Western e-commerce brands is a strong plus



What We Offer



• Competitive salary based on experience

• Direct collaboration with the CEO

• A fast-growing company where your impact is immediately visible

• Room to grow in salary and responsibility as the company scales



How to Apply



Send your application with “Olvy” at the beginning of your subject title, along with a short introduction, your relevant experience, and examples of products you have sourced or compared in the past. Show us how you think and how you work.



We look forward to hearing from you.

title: Closer   /// Sales-Focused Virtual Assistant / Closer
description:
???? WE’RE HIRING SALES CLOSERS ????



If you’re confident on the phone, hungry to earn, and ready to close real leads — this is for you.



We’re hosting group interviews to bring on a small number of qualified closers who want:

? Consistent opportunities

? High-commission potential

? A proven system (no guessing, no fluff)

? Growth with a serious team

If you know you can close, this is your shot. ????

We are seeking a driven Closer who wants real ownership, not just a task list. This isn't about managing a CRM—it's about being the high-energy engine of our sales pipeline. If you thrive on accountability, hate micromanagement, and want a paycheck that reflects your performance, this long-term, full-time remote role is for you.



Your Mission: To own the "middle-to-end" of the sales funnel. Your primary goal is to turn leads into revenue by building pipeline, running compelling demos, and closing deals on the spot.



What You’ll Do:

Prospect & Build Pipeline: Make 75–100+ outbound/cold calls daily. You don't wait for perfect leads—you create them.



Demo & Close: Run live demos (showing how to get an "instant pay raise") and confidently ask for the sale, converting prospects into paying customers.



Manage Leads: Follow up diligently on warm leads and inbound requests.



CRM Mastery: Fully manage our GoHighLevel (GHL) CRM—maintain a clean pipeline, update stages, and execute SMS/email follow-up sequences.



Onboard & Support: Help new customers get live by setting up the product to their specific needs.



Drive Improvement: Share feedback on objections and blockers. Work with the team to refine messaging and improve win rates.



Administrative Execution: Schedule appointments and ensure impeccable daily CRM hygiene.



What "Success" Looks Like

Closing 20+ new paying customers weekly.



Maintaining an impeccable, accurate GHL pipeline.



Consistently improving close rates and conversion over time.



Who You Are (Requirements):

title: Meta Ads VSL Video Editor Skincare B-Roll & Direct Response
description:
READ THIS FIRST

We hire based on execution, not resumes.



We are looking for a Meta Ads video editor who can turn detailed ad briefings, voice-overs, scripts, and B-roll directions into high-converting skincare creatives.



This is not a basic video editing role.



You need to understand direct response editing, VSL-style ads, B-roll driven storytelling, hook pacing, visual retention, and how Meta ads need to feel in the first 3 seconds.



You will start with a paid test video.



Paid test structure:



Up to 60 seconds: $5

Over 60 seconds: $10



If your test is strong, we are looking for a long-term part-time editor, with the possibility to grow into a full-time or lead editor role.



ABOUT US



We are building ELYVIA, a premium skincare brand focused on at-home infusion treatments.



Our hero product is a systtem designed for women who want smoother-looking, firmer-looking and more refined skin from home.



Our ads focus on topics such as:



sagging jowls and lower-face ageing

acne scarring and uneven texture

under-eye bags

skincare products only working at the surface level

clinic treatments being expensive, inconvenient, or too invasive



We create performance-driven Meta ads for the UK market.



You will receive detailed scripts, voice-overs, and structured briefings. For example, our current ad briefings include full scripts, timed B-roll scenes, product shots, animation needs, before/after visuals, and direct-response structure for creatives.



We need an editor who can take this level of briefing and turn it into a polished, high-converting video ad.



WHAT YOU’LL DO



You will edit Meta ads using:



VSL-style editing

B-roll storytelling

product visuals

AI UGC or avatar footage when needed

skincare mechanism animations

before/after proof visuals

subtitles and retention-focused text

sound design and pacing

clean mobile-first 9:16 formatting



You will receive:



finished scripts

voice-over files

detailed scene-by-scene briefings

access to our B-roll library

product assets

examples of previous ads



We expect you to source additional visuals yourself when the briefing requires something we do not  have.



For example, if the script mentions collagen support, micro-channels, peptide serum delivery, acne scar texture, clinic microneedling, jawline sagging, or skin-layer animations, you need to be able to find, create, or source suitable visuals that match the message.



Some of our scripts require shots every 2–4 seconds, with longer animations where needed for mechanism explanations.



YOU MUST BE ABLE TO

Edit high-converting Meta ads, not just nice-looking videos

Understand direct response creative structure

Build VSL-style ads from a voice-over

Match visuals tightly to every line of the script

Source strong B-roll when our library does not cover a scene

Create or source simple skincare animations

Add clean subtitles and on-screen text

Improve pacing, retention, and visual clarity

Make premium skincare ads that feel trustworthy, not cheap or dropshipping

Work independently without needing every shot explained twice

Follow a detailed briefing while still bringing creative input



REQUIRED EXPERIENCE



You should have experience with at least one of these:



Meta ad creatives

VSL ads

direct response video ads

UGC-style ads

ecommerce product ads

skincare / beauty / wellness ads

B-roll driven video ads



Experience with skincare, beauty, health, wellness, or direct-response ecommerce is a strong plus.



SOFTWARE / TOOLS



You must have your own editing software and tools.



Examples:



Adobe Premiere Pro

After Effects

CapCut Pro

DaVinci Resolve

Final Cut Pro

Motion graphics / animation tools

stock footage platforms

AI video tools if relevant



We do not provide editing software. You are expected to already have the tools you need.



IMPORTANT SKILLS



We are specifically looking for someone who understands:



how to make the first 3 seconds scroll-stopping

how to keep visual pacing tight

how to make scripts easy to follow visually

how to source relevant B-roll

how to create visual contrast between “problem” and “solution”

how to edit before/after proof sections

how to use animations for mechanism explanation

how to make skincare ads look premium and credible

how to edit for Meta ads, not YouTube or long-form content



HOW WE WORK



We give clear direction.



You will usually receive:



script

voice-over

example competitor ad

scene-by-scene briefing

B-roll notes

brand direction

product assets

access to our B-roll library



But we do not want someone who only places the exact files we provide.



A strong editor should be able to look at the script, understand the emotional and conversion goal, and source missing visuals where needed.



If a scene says “skin-layer collagen support animation” or “woman 50+ touching sagging jawline area,” we expect you to understand what visual is needed and find or create something that fits.



COMPENSATION



We start with a paid test.



Paid test structure:



Test video up to 60 seconds: $5

Test video over 60 seconds: $10



After the test, if the quality is strong, we discuss ongoing work.



Long-term compensation can include:



part-time fixed pay

consistent weekly editing workload

bonuses for winning creatives

higher pay as performance improves

potential full-time or lead editor role



We are not looking for a one-off freelancer. We are looking for someone who can grow with the brand.



TEST VIDEO



For the test, you will receive:



a script

voice-over

example ad / reference

access to base assets

detailed creative briefing

B-roll notes



You are expected to create a complete Meta ad.



We will evaluate:



hook strength

pacing

visual matching

B-roll sourcing

sound design

structure

clarity

premium skincare feel

ability to follow the briefing

ability to improve the creative with your own judgement



HOW TO APPLY



SEND:

Your portfolio

Examples of Meta ads, VSL ads, UGC ads, or ecommerce ads you edited

What editing software you use

Whether you can source B-roll yourself

Whether you can create or source simple skincare / skin-layer animations

How you approach the first 3 seconds of a Meta ad



IMPORTANT



Please only apply if you are comfortable working with detailed ad briefings and performance-focused scripts.



Do not apply if:



you only do basic cuts

you cannot source visuals yourself

you do not understand Meta ad pacing

you cannot work from a structured briefing

you do not have your own editing software

you cannot show a relevant portfolio



If you meet most of the requirements but not all, you can still apply. Just be honest about your skill level and show us your best work.



We are happy to train the right person, but you must already have a strong editing foundation and a good eye for ads.







Kind regards,



Syfax.

title: Executive Virtual Assistant
description:
Skyride Auto Services is a growing car rental company based in Columbus, Ohio, focused on providing affordable, reliable vehicles to gig drivers. We specialize in flexible rental terms, no credit checks, and high-touch customer service. As we continue to scale, we’re looking for a proactive and highly organized Executive Virtual Assistant to help manage daily operations and support business growth.



Role Overview



As our Executive Virtual Assistant, you’ll play a critical role in keeping the business running smoothly. You’ll handle day-to-day administrative and operational tasks while actively engaging potential customers with rental offers. This role is ideal for a self-starter who is detail-oriented, comfortable with outreach, and able to work independently in a fast-paced environment. This is a full time role. Must be willing to work during US business hours.



Job Description:

As the Executive Virtual Assistant, you will manage the essential functions of our car rental business and take an active role in offering rental deals to prospective clients. The ideal candidate should be a go-getter with exceptional organizational skills and the ability to work independently.



Key Responsibilities:

 - Lead Generation & Offers: Make a minimum of 50 daily rental offers to prospective customers through phone, or social media platforms.

 - CRM Management: Manage and update customer data in CRM platforms (Go High Level, ensuring accurate records and seamless customer follow-ups.

 - Customer Support: Respond to customer inquiries and resolve issues promptly via
, phone, and social media.

 - Reservation Management: Manage reservations, confirm bookings, and assist with pick-up/drop-off coordination.

 - Fleet Management: Track vehicle availability, ensure timely maintenance, and manage any logistical issues.

 - Billing & Payments: Process payments, generate invoices, and track all financial records.

 - Administrative Tasks: Data entry, document organization, and management of all rental records.

 - Scheduling: Coordinate customer appointments, vehicle maintenance, and other business-related schedules.

 - Social Media Management: Assist with social media posts, manage online listings, and respond to customer reviews.

 - Customer Communication: Regularly communicate updates, offers, and notices to customers.



Requirements

 - Previous experience as a Virtual Assistant or Executive Assistant (preferred)

 - Comfortable making daily outreach and rental offers

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills

 - Experience with CRM platforms (Go High Level preferred)

 - Excellent organization, attention to detail, and time-management skills

 - Ability to multitask and work independently

 - Proficient with Google Workspace, Microsoft Office, and social media platforms

 - Experience in car rentals, transportation, or the gig economy is a plus



What We Offer

 - Fully remote work

 - Flexible hours

 - Opportunity to grow with a fast-scaling business

 - Hands-on experience in a niche car rental market



How to Apply

If you’re proactive, reliable, and ready to grow with us, please send:

 - A short video introduction

 - Your resume

 - A cover letter explaining why you’re a strong fit

Send to:

title: Content Operations Manager — Personal Brand + Community
description:
CONTENT OPERATIONS MANAGER — PERSONAL BRAND + COMMUNITY

Full-time | Remote | Philippines | $1,000+ USD/month | PST hours + weekends



ABOUT THIS ROLE



This is a full-time, exclusive role supporting a globally recognized finance educator and content creator with 450K+ LinkedIn followers, a 40K+ newsletter, and a growing online Academy and executive community. You will be the operational backbone of how content gets created, published, and distributed — and how a high-value community of executives experiences our platform every day.



This is not a 9-to-5 role and it is not for someone who needs to be managed. It is for someone who runs toward problems, operates with urgency, and uses AI not as a novelty but as the foundation of how they work. If you got comfortable in your last role, this is the wrong opportunity. If you are always looking for a faster, smarter way to do what you did yesterday, keep reading.



PLATFORMS



Circle — spaces, events, member management, moderation



Content publishing



LinkedIn — native publishing, scheduling, engagement, analytics

Beehiiv — newsletter drafting, scheduling, segmentation, analytics

Thinkific — course publishing, student communication, basic back-end



Content ops



A content calendar tool: Notion, Airtable, or ClickUp

Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Drive) or Microsoft 365

Loom — async video communication



AI tools (daily use, multiple)



ChatGPT / Claude / Gemini

At least one image tool: Midjourney, Ideogram, or DALL·E

At least one workflow/automation: 
, Zapier, or n8n



Design + repurposing



Canva — graphics, carousels, templates

A video repurposing tool: Descript, Opus Clip, or CapCut



Technical



HTML/CSS — able to read, fix, and deploy

A deployment target: Netlify, GitHub Pages, Vercel, or Webflow

Basic code editor: VS Code, Cursor, or similar



Work environment



Azure Virtual Desktop (AVD) — or equivalent remote-desktop work history



Bonus / nice to have



Scheduling layer: Nuelink

Perplexity, Notion AI, ElevenLabs

Figma, Descript for editing

Teams fluency

Google Analytics / basic funnel reading

SEO Knowledge



WHAT YOU WILL DO



- Manage our Circle community end-to-end — onboarding, events, engagement loops, member experience, and day-to-day moderation across multiple spaces and tiers

- Coordinate content across LinkedIn, our newsletter (Beehiiv), the Academy (Thinkific), and our brands — scheduling, repurposing, and publishing with zero errors

- Maintain and execute a content calendar across both personal brand and company brand 

- Troubleshoot and deploy HTML — fix broken elements, update landing pages, push changes to live environments without waiting for a developer

- Build and improve AI-assisted workflows to increase output volume and reduce turnaround time — and proactively replace anything you built last month with something better this month

- Manage event logistics inside Circle — live sessions, replays, announcements, and follow-up sequences

- Flag issues before they escalate. Surface problems your employer doesn't know about yet. Be two steps ahead, always.



WHAT WE REQUIRE 



- Demonstrated experience managing content for a personal brand with a significant following — LinkedIn, YouTube, newsletter, or similar. We will verify this. Vague claims will disqualify you.

- Proven experience managing an online community platform (Circle, Mighty Networks, Slack, 
) at an operational level — not just participation

- Ability to troubleshoot and fix HTML/CSS issues and deploy to live environments. This will be tested.

- Fluent, professional English — written and spoken. This role involves executive-facing communication.

- AI-native work style — you can demonstrate specific workflows you have built and measurably improved over time. "I use ChatGPT" is not sufficient.

- Available full-time, exclusively — no other clients, no side projects that compete for your attention during work hours

- Available during PST business hours and on weekends as needed. This is a high-output role.

- Minimum 2 years in a single role with a verifiable reference who will answer the phone



WHO YOU ARE



- You move fast and think clearly at the same time — speed without sloppiness

- You are organized by default, not by effort — your systems are tighter than your employer expects

- You take initiative and solve problems before reporting them — not the other way around

- You are loyal. You do not treat this as a stepping stone. You build something and you own it.

- You understand personal branding mechanics at a deep level — what makes content perform, why audiences grow, how community trust is built

- You are comfortable working in a monitored environment with tracked hours and clear output expectations — and you prefer it that way because you have nothing to hide

- You do not need to be the most creative person in the room. You need to be the most reliable and the fastest executor in the room.



COMPENSATION



$1,000+ USD per month depending on experience and demonstrated results. After a successful 90-day trial, a performance bonus structure applies based on output targets. This is a contractor arrangement. You are responsible for your own taxes and benefits. 





HOW TO APPLY



Read every instruction carefully. Applications that skip steps or ignore instructions are automatically disqualified — this is intentional.



Record a 90-second Loom video — unedited, screen share on. Open your AI tool of choice and walk us through one workflow you have built that saves you real time. Talk while you do it. Do not prepare a script.



Answer these three questions in 3 sentences each — no more:

   - "Describe a time a person you worked for made a decision you disagreed with. What did you do?"

   - "What is one thing you stopped doing in your work in the last 6 months because AI made it faster or obsolete?"

   - "Working 9–10 hours a day, including some weekends, is a real requirement of this role. What does that mean for your life right now — be honest."



Provide the following links — all are required:

   - Your LinkedIn profile 

   - One example of a community or social account you have managed — link to the platform

   - One piece of content you personally wrote in your own voice — a post, article, or newsletter issue



Provide one professional reference — a former employer or client (not a colleague) — with their name, role, and a phone number or
 we can use to contact them before any interview.



NOTE ON THE PROCESS: Shortlisted applicants will be asked to complete a paid test task ($50 USD, approximately 2.5 hours). This task includes content repurposing, an HTML fix with live deployment, a community scenario response, and an AI workflow design exercise. A screen recording is required for the duration of the task. Finalists will have a 30-minute live video interview. The role includes a 30-day paid trial period with defined output targets.





```

To apply share your linkedin, work samples as appicable, break down and rate your experience and skill level across each area and tools.

title: HR/Admin Virtual Assistant
description:
About Us

MSE Environmental is a growing environmental consulting company operating across Nevada, Texas, Arizona, and future U.S. markets. We provide asbestos, mold, lead, environmental site assessments, compliance testing, and related environmental services.

We are looking for a highly organized, detail-oriented virtual assistant based in the Philippines to support our HR, admin, payroll preparation, invoicing coordination, and internal documentation systems.

This role is ideal for someone who is dependable, proactive, excellent with details, and comfortable working inside systems like Notion, Gusto, Google Workspace, and project management tools.



Role Overview

The HR/Admin VA will help keep our internal operations organized and moving. You will not be responsible for making HR decisions, handling employee discipline, or giving legal/compliance advice. Instead, your role is to help organize requests, track tasks, update systems, prepare information, follow up on missing documents, and support the leadership team.

You will work closely with company leadership, fractional HR support, bookkeeping, invoicing, and team managers. 



Key Responsibilities

HR / People Admin Support

Track PTO requests and update the PTO tracker

Maintain employee contact lists and internal directories

Help organize onboarding and offboarding checklists

Follow up on missing employee documents

Maintain employee file checklists

Assist with HR inbox organization

Prepare weekly HR/admin summaries for leadership

Track employee training completion

Update Notion HR dashboards and databases

Organize SOPs, Loom videos, and training materials



Payroll Preparation Support

Prepare payroll checklist before payroll dates

Confirm time entries or missing payroll items

Coordinate reminders with tea
mbers

Help collect payroll-related updates

Organize payroll questions for leadership or fractional HR

Track recurring payroll deadlines

Final payroll approval will remain with company leadership or authorized U.S.-based payroll personnel.



Invoicing / Collections Admin Support

Help organize invoice status updates

Assist with tracking unpaid invoices

Update invoicing dashboards

Send internal reminders about missing invoice information

Help coordinate between estimators, consultants, bookkeeper, and collections team

Maintain simple A/R tracking reports as needed



Notion / Systems Support

Build and maintain Notion databases

Update task lists, checklists, SOP pages, and dashboards

Organize company documentation

Convert Loom videos into written SOP drafts using AI tools

Keep recurring tasks visible and updated

Help maintain a clean operating system for HR, payroll, invoicing, and admin tasks



Required Skills

Excellent written English

Strong attention to detail

Highly organized and reliable

Experience with Google Sheets and Google Drive

Comfortable using Notion or willing to learn quickly

Able to follow SOPs and checklists

Able to maintain confidentiality

Good communication and follow-up habits

Comfortable working with U.S.-based teams

Able to work independently without constant supervision



Preferred Experience 

3 Years HR admin support - Required

Payroll preparation support

Bamboohr

Gusto or similar payroll platforms

QuickBooks or invoicing systems

Notion

Google Workspace

CRM systems

Environmental, construction, restoration, or service businesses

Collections or A/R tracking

SOP documentation



What Success Looks Like

You will be successful in this role if:

PTO requests are tracked clearly

Payroll prep items are organized before deadlines

Employee documents are not missing or forgotten

Notion dashboards stay updated

Invoicing/admin tasks are followed up on

Leadership receives clear weekly summaries

Tasks do not disappear

The team feels more organized because of your support



Important Notes

This is an admin support role. You will not be expected to make final HR decisions, approve payroll, discipline employees, or handle legal compliance decisions. Your job is to organize, track, remind, document, and support the team.

We are looking for someone who is dependable, steady, honest, and willing to grow with the company.



How to Apply

Please send:

Your resume

A short introduction about your experience

Your hourly rate in USD

Your availability and preferred work schedule

A short Loom video introducing yourself and explaining why you would be a good fit

Examples of any admin, HR, Notion, payroll, or invoicing work you have done

Use the subject line:

HR/Admin VA Application — [Your Name]

. We are planning a structured migration to C# with improved architecture and scalability, while maintaining and extending the system.

Understand an existing system

Migrate it properly (not just rewrite blindly)

Improve structure, performance, and maintainability

Develop new modules over time



This is a long-term opportunity, starting on a part-time / project basis.



Tech Stack


 (existing system)

C# (target platform)

WPF (MVVM architecture)

SQL (SQLite ? SQL Server migration planned)

Development Tools

Experience using modern AI-assisted development tools is required

Familiarity with GitHub Copilot is strongly preferred

A Copilot Pro subscription is expected for this role (can be provided if required)



Responsibilities

Analyse and understand the existing 
 BMS

Plan and execute migration to C# (modular, structured approach)

Refactor and improve architecture where required

Develop new modules and features over time

Work with database structures and data migration

Maintain stability of the live system during transition

Follow structured and maintainable coding practices



Requirements

Strong experience with:



C#

WPF (MVVM pattern)

Proven experience working with existing / legacy systems (non-negotiable)

Experience with system migration or refactoring projects

Strong understanding of:

Desktop application architecture

Database design and SQL



Ability to:

Read and understand existing code

Propose structured improvements (not over-engineering)

Strong problem-solving ability

Clear and reliable communication



Nice to Have (Not Required)

Experience migrating SQLite ? SQL Server

Experience working with Australian companies



How to Apply

To apply, please include:

A short introduction (max 5 sentences)

A brief description of a system you have worked on (not small apps)

A short explanation of how you would approach migrating a 
 system to C#

Growth Opportunity

title: Data Annotator / Labeler
description:
About Us:

We're a US startup building intelligence solutions to revolutionize how contractors design and build in the 21st century. As our work is growing in adoption across North America and Europe, we're currently looking for a Data Annotator to join our expanding remote team. This is a 40 hours/week contractor role with long-term potential for direct full-time employment.



Your Role:

As a Data Annotator, you’ll be responsible for turning raw data into high-quality, labeled datasets that power customer reports and AI tools for progress tracking, safety compliance, worksite estimation, and more.



Key Responsibilities:

* Annotate and tag construction-related data (e.g., videos, images, blueprints, documents)

* Identify and classify structures, machinery, safety gear, materials and site activities

* Ensure accuracy, consistency, and completeness of data labeling based on detailed project guidelines

* Collaborate with QA specialists and project managers for review cycles and feedback

* Deliver high-quality labeled datasets on a defined schedule



What We’re Looking For:

- Meticulous attention to detail and high accuracy standards

- Basic familiarity with construction terms or visual elements (helpful, but training is provided)

- Ability to follow complex, detailed instructions and work independently

- Excellent written and conversational English skills for collaborating with teammates

- Comfortable working with our labeling tools and communication platforms (Slack, GSuite, Google Sheets, Zoom)

- Stable internet connection and personal computer/laptop capable of running Chrome with reliable HD video streaming



Experience in data labeling, BPO, architecture, engineering, or construction is a plus!



What We Offer:

* Work with a global team shaping the future of construction technology

* Competitive pay with performance-based incentives and clear pay/skill progression

* 100% remote work with flexible scheduling hours

* Training and upskilling in AI and data operations

* Potential to grow with us into QA, project management, or technical roles



Ready to Join Us?

If you're detail-driven, curious about technology, and excited by the idea of supporting smarter construction worldwide -- we want to hear from you!



Apply now by sending your CV. Please be prepared for a rapid interview cycle as we are seeking several immediate placements.



(BONUS: We will prioritize applicants who additionally provide a short 2-min. video (Loom, unlisted YouTube, Drive, Dropbox) link introducing yourself and any prior data labeling experience.)

title: Receptionist / Customer Service Representative (Remote)
description:
Are you someone who stays calm under pressure, communicates clearly, and enjoys helping people the 
nt they call in?



Peninsula Insurance Services is hiring a Remote Receptionist / Customer Service Tea
mber to become the front-line voice of our agency.



You’ll begin by answering inbound calls, helping clients, scheduling appointments, and keeping communication flowing smoothly. As you learn our systems and processes, you’ll have the opportunity to grow into a larger customer service role over time.



What You’ll Do:



Deliver excellent service to clients and prospects

Manage inbound calls and voi
 messages

Document communications in our systems accurately

Relay messages and inquiries to the appropriate tea
mbers

Help process payments, policy changes, and service requests

Send insurance documents to clients and related parties

Support day-to-day non-licensed customer service tasks



What We’re Looking For:



- Strong customer service experience

- Clear spoken and written English

- Calm under pressure when phones are busy

- Detail-oriented and dependable

- Strong computer skills and ability to learn new systems

- Reliable internet connection and quiet workspace

- Laptop or desktop setup (dual monitors preferred)

- Available Monday through Friday during US business hours



What We Offer:



- Starting pay of $150 to $200 per week based on experience, paid every Friday

- Paid time off

- Paid US federal holidays

- Full-time, long-term work from home position

- Monday through Friday, 8:30 AM to 5:30 PM Eastern Time

- Paid training on our systems and insurance processes

- Clear growth path into expanded customer service responsibilities

- Stable team environment with long-term opportunities

title: Social Media Content Creator + Manager (Part-Time ? Full-Time)
description:
ABOUT GUD BASEBALL

GUD Baseball (Ground Up Development) is a pitching velocity development company founded by a former MLB pitcher who went from the upper 80s to consistently touching 100+ mph. We've trained 450+ athletes remotely, with 175+ breaking the 90 mph threshold.

Our mission: give serious pitchers the exact blueprint to add real, measurable velocity — no gimmicks, no conventional lesson model. We're growing fast and need a content creator who can help us reach the athletes who need us most.



POSITION OVERVIEW

This role starts part-time (approximately 20 hours/week) with a clear path to full-time based on performance and output quality. We send content in batches — not on a rigid daily schedule — so you'll need to be self-directed, manage your own pipeline, and deliver consistently without being micromanaged.



WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING



Edit raw coaching clips, athlete training footage, and screen recordings into short-form content optimized for each platform

Write hooks, captions, and CTAs that stop the scroll and drive engagement for a 16–26 year old pitcher audience

Research and apply platform-specific hashtag strategies to maximize organic reach and follower growth

Identify the best posting times for our target market and maintain a consistent publishing schedule

Repurpose long-form content into platform-native clips across all managed channels

Source or create supplemental content to fill the calendar between raw footage batches

Track weekly performance metrics (views, follows, engagement rate) and bring improvement recommendations

Own the content calendar and coordinate with our team around promotions, webinars, and program launches





PLATFORMS YOU'LL MANAGE

TikTok | Instagram Reels | YouTube Shorts | X 
) |
 | LinkedIn



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR



Proven short-form video editing experience — a portfolio or reel with at least 3 examples is required to be considered

Strong understanding of what actually performs on TikTok and Instagram Reels (hooks, pacing, sound, text overlays)

Ability to recommend your own tool stack — we're open to your suggestions (CapCut, OpusClip, Metricool, etc.)

Experience managing multiple social platforms simultaneously with a consistent brand voice

Strong written English — you'll be writing captions, hooks, and hashtag sets independently

Self-directed with clear communication — you'll manage your own output pipeline between content batches

Interest in sports, fitness, or athletic performance is a genuine plus

Comfortable pulling basic analytics and reporting what's working





COMPENSATION

$3–$5/hr USD depending on experience

Starting part-time (~20 hrs/week) with a path to full-time based on performance



HOW TO APPLY

To be considered, your application must include all of the following:



A link to your portfolio, reel, or at least 3 examples of short-form content you've created or edited

3–5 sentences on why you're the right fit for a sports/performance brand targeting young athletes

A list of the tools you currently use for video editing, scheduling, and analytics — and what you'd recommend we use



Applications without a portfolio will not be reviewed.

Use the subject line: GUD Content Creator — [Your Name]

title: Senior Video Editor (Luxury Real Estate)
description:
Senior Video Editor (Luxury Real Estate)



Full-Time Position



We are a luxury real estate media company seeking a full-time Senior Video Editor who specializes in crafting high-end, cinematic property films. This role is for a highly skilled professional who understands storytelling, pacing, and premium color—and can consistently deliver polished, luxury-level video content.



About the Role



As our Senior Video Editor, you will be responsible for editing a steady volume of luxury real estate videos, including cinematic property tours, agent-led videos, and brand storytelling content.



Every project should reflect a refined, high-end aesthetic—clean edits, intentional pacing, natural yet elevated color grading, and seamless motion. This is not a template-driven role. We value precision, consistency, and creative craftsmanship.



Required Expertise

- 8+ years of professional video editing experience (senior level)

- Strong background in:

- Luxury real estate video editing

- Cinematic property tours

- Agent/on-camera videos

- Story-driven brand content



Advanced expertise in:

- High-end color grading (natural, clean, luxury tone)

- DaVinci Resolve (required)

- Speed ramping and motion timing

- Music-driven editing and pacing

- Clean, professional captioning for social and branding



Deep understanding of:

- Visual storytelling and narrative flow

- Shot selection and sequencing

- Matching footage across multiple cameras and lighting conditions

- Maintaining consistency across projects and clients



Technical Requirements / Advanced proficiency in:

- DaVinci Resolve (primary editing platform)

- Strong working knowledge of:

- Color management and node-based grading workflows

- Export settings for social media, MLS, and high-end marketing use



Strictly no reliance on:

AI-generated edits

Auto-cutting or template-based platforms



What We’re Looking For



A dedicated full-time editor—not a freelancer splitting attention.

Someone who understands luxury pacing—when to slow down, when to build energy

Strong attention to detail (no sloppy cuts, mismatched color, or over-editing)

Ability to create emotion-driven edits, not just compile clips

Reliable communication and consistent turnaround times

Alignment with a premium, luxury brand aesthetic



Nice to Have

Experience editing $1M+ luxury listings

Familiarity with real estate marketing trends and agent branding

Experience working with drone footage and gimbal-based walkthroughs



What Success Looks Like



Videos feel intentional, cinematic, and elevated

Agents look natural, confident, and well-paced on camera

Every project reinforces a luxury brand standard

Consistent quality across high volume without sacrificing detail

title: Short-Form Video Editor — AI Prank Reels (TikTok / IG Reels / YouTube Shorts)
description:
About the role: We're producing viral AI-powered prank videos for TikTok/Reels and need a fast, creative editor who understands the platform. Each video has 3 pre-made segments that you'll assemble and polish:



Hook — AI-generated video (the shocking reveal that stops the scroll)

App demo — Screen recording showing how the prank was made

Chat reaction — iPhone iMessage screen recording of the victim's response

What you'll do:



Assemble the 3 clips into one cohesive vertical reel (9:16)

Add bold, attention-grabbing captions/text overlays (CapCut or Canva style)

Time the text to match the conversation pacing

Add trending sounds / background music

Color correct and match the segments so they flow seamlessly

Optimize the first 1-2 seconds for maximum hook/retention

Export at 1080x1920, ready to post

You're a great fit if you:



Have experience editing short-form viral content (TikTok, Reels, Shorts)

Understand pacing, retention, and what makes people stop scrolling

Can match the editing style of top-performing prank/AI content creators

Know how to use CapCut, Premiere Pro, or similar

Can turn around edits fast (same day / next day)

Have a portfolio of short-form content with strong view counts

Volume: ~7-10 videos per batch, ongoing work for the right person. We provide all raw assets — you bring the editing magic.



To apply: Send 2-3 examples of short-form content you've edited (prank or reaction style preferred). Bonus if you can do a test edit with sample clips we provide.

What I'm looking for

- Reliable with deadlines — this is a recurring job and consistency matters

- Interest in men's fashion is a big plus — helps you understand the aesthetic we're going for



Please include "outfit graphic" in your application so I know you read this fully



Start: ASAP

title: Full-Time Growth, Content & Marketing Operator
description:
Full-Time Growth, Content & Marketing Operator --

(Lead Generation + Video + SEO + Systems + Training Development)



I am looking for a high-level operator to help grow Business Fuzion and support client outcomes through content, marketing, and structured execution.



This is not a traditional “marketing” role, and it is not a basic assistant position.



This role sits at the intersection of:



* Growth and lead generation

* Content creation and video editing

* SEO and AI-driven visibility

* Marketing problem-solving for clients

* Training development and content organization



Your primary responsibility is to drive attention, generate inbound leads, and help build and organize the systems and training our clients rely on to succeed.



---



Primary Objective



Turn attention into leads ? turn leads into booked appointments ? support systems and training that help convert, onboard, and retain those clients.



---



Core Responsibilities



Growth & Lead Generation (Primary Focus):



* Drive inbound leads for Business Fuzion through content, distribution, and creative strategy

* Increase visibility, reach, and brand awareness

* Identify where attention exists and position content to capture it

* Support funnel performance and booked appointment flow



Content Creation & Video Editing:



* Edit and produce short-form content (TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts) daily

* Edit long-form content (YouTube, training videos, internal content)

* Repurpose one piece of content into multiple assets

* Optimize content for retention, engagement, and performance



SEO, AI & Content Leverage:



* Use SEO and AI tools to expand reach and discoverability

* Create high-quality, strategic articles and website content (focused on intent, not volume)

* Optimize existing content for better performance

* Balance short-term attention (social) with long-term growth (search)



Training Program Development & Client Content:



* Take raw Zoom recordings, walkthroughs, and internal knowledge and turn them into structured training programs

* Edit and package training videos into clean, professional modules

* Organize training into clear, step-by-step systems clients can easily follow

* Continuously improve and expand the training experience based on client needs

* Help build a scalable client education system (modules, lessons, onboarding content)

* Ensure every client can log in and clearly understand how to use their system without confusion



Marketing Fixes (Client Support – As Needed):



* Identify and fix key marketing gaps for clients

* Improve listings, tracking, visibility, and basic conversion issues

* Address “revenue leaks” such as missed engagement, lack of follow-up, or poor visibility

* Support improvements that increase lead flow and conversion



---



How You Think



* You understand that attention is the foundation of growth

* You move quickly and execute consistently

* You are both creative and analytical

* You take ownership and solve problems without waiting for instructions

* You think in systems, not isolated tasks

* You focus on results, not just activity



---



What You Must Be Strong At



* Video editing (CapCut, Premiere, Final Cut, or similar)

* Short-form content strategy and execution

* Understanding of how content drives leads and revenue

* Ability to repurpose and scale content using AI

* Strong organizational skills (especially for structuring training content)

* Ability to turn unstructured ideas into clean, usable systems

* Basic understanding of marketing systems (funnels, listings, tracking, conversion)



---



This Role is NOT for Someone Who



* Only wants to post on social media

* Needs step-by-step instructions for everything

* Focuses on design over performance

* Works slowly or over-perfects content

* Does not understand how marketing connects to revenue



---



This IS for Someone Who



* Can take raw ideas and turn them into high-performing content

* Can take complex information and turn it into simple, structured training

* Understands how to capture attention and convert it into an opportunity

* Can think independently and improve what they touch

* Wants to grow with a company long-term

* Takes pride in building systems and training that actually work



---



This is a full-time, long-term position.



You will be working directly with me to grow the business, improve marketing performance, and build both the external content and internal training systems that drive client success.



This is not about completing tasks.



This is about building something that works — and scales.



Application Instructions (Required)



To be considered for this role, you must complete the steps below. Applications without these will not be reviewed.



This is designed to understand how you think, create, and execute.



---



1. Content Thinking (Creativity + Lead Generation)



Give me 10 short-form content ideas that would generate leads for a business like mine (Business Fuzion).



These should:



* Grab attention quickly

* Be specific (not generic motivation)

* Be designed to convert viewers into interested prospects



---



2. Video Editing Sample (Execution)



Take any short video clip (your own or sample content) and:



* Edit it into a 30–60 second short-form video

* Add captions

* Make it engaging for TikTok / Reels / Shorts



Send a Loom or video file showing your final edit.



---



3. Training Organization (Systems Thinking)



Explain how you would take a 30–60 minute Zoom call and turn it into a clean training module for clients.



Include:



* How you would break it down into sections

* How you would name and organize the content

* How you would make it easy for a client to follow



---



4. Marketing Awareness (Problem Solving)



A local business has:



* No SEO

* No tracking (pixels, analytics)

* Poor listings

* No response to reviews



What would you fix FIRST and why?



---



5. AI + Efficiency (Leverage)



What AI tools would you use to:



* Speed up content creation

* Improve output

* Repurpose content



Explain briefly how you would use them.



---



6. Short Loom Video (Communication + Thinking)



Record a 3–5 minute Loom explaining:



* How would you generate leads for Business Fuzion using content

* How you would structure your weekly workflow



---



IMPORTANT:



I am not looking for perfect answers.



I am looking for:



* Clear thinking

* Creativity

* Speed

* Ownership

* Ability to execute without constant direction



Only candidates who complete all steps will be considered.

title: [Fast Hiring] Virtual Receptionist VA (Remote) | 24-Hour Review
description:
Important: Our application process moves very quickly. If you are selected for the next rounds of interviews, you must be able to respond promptly. Candidates who do not respond in a timely manner may be passed over.



We are currently seeking a Remote Spa Receptionist. This role consists of handling inbound calls, booking appointments, and completing various administrative tasks. This is a long-term, performance-based position. All calls are recorded and reviewed regularly. Continued placement depends on accuracy, professionalism, booking success, reliable attendance, no missed calls during scheduled shifts, quick response times, and the ability to professionally manage complaints and de-escalate dissatisfied clients.



For candidates who may be a stronger fit outside of a voice-based role, we also offer non-voice positions with a heavier focus on administrative tasks, subject to availability and overall fit.



To apply: Please copy the questions below, paste them into your reply, and answer each question thoroughly.



(1) Location & Utilities: Where in the Philippines are you currently located? How often have you experienced power or internet outages in the past year? Do you have backup solutions (e.g., generator, mobile hotspot) to ensure your work is not affected?



(2) Home Setup: Will you be working from home? Is your workspace quiet and suitable for handling calls? Do you have your own computer or laptop? Please attach a screenshot of your current internet speed.



(3) Availability & Speed: Can you consistently work seven days a week, six hours per day, and remain available for the full shift without missed calls? How quickly do you typically answer inbound calls? What is your current typing speed in words per minute?



(4) Call & Complaint Experience: Describe your experience with answering inbound calls, booking or scheduling appointments, customer service, following scripts, and handling complaints. Provide one real example of how you de-escalated an upset client and the outcome.



(5) Tools & Systems: What programs, tools, phone systems, or VoIP platforms have you used for handling calls? Do you have any issues with background noise, power outages, or internet interruptions? How do you take notes and log client interactions during calls?



(6) Voice Recordings: Please submit two short voice recordings. Recording one should demonstrate booking an appointment from 7:00 PM to 8:00 PM with a total amount of $60. Recording two should demonstrate handling a double charge for a 90-minute massage where the client sees two $90 charges and is requesting a refund. Your tone should be calm, polite, and professional.



(7) Current Job: Do you currently have another job? If yes, where, how many hours per week, and what are your roles and tasks in that job?



(8) Schedule, Pay & Start Date: Are you able to work seven days a week, six hours per day, during Central Standard Time (CST) hours without conflicts? What is your expected hourly or monthly rate? What is your earliest physical start date if hired?



(9) Marketing, Design & Video: Do you have experience with online marketing, creating content using Canva, or producing short video clips such as
 Reels or similar platforms? Please describe your skills and provide samples of your previous work if available.



(10) Fit, Skills & Experience: Briefly introduce yourself and explain why you would be a good fit for this role. Include what you believe is most important when handling spa clients over the phone, especially during complaints, and what other skills or areas you have a lot of experience in.

For candidates who may be a stronger fit outside of a voice-based role, we also offer non-voice positions with a heavier focus on administrative tasks, subject to availability and overall fit.



To apply: Please copy the questions below, paste them into your reply, and answer each question thoroughly.



(1) Location & Utilities: Where in the Philippines are you currently located? How often have you experienced power or internet outages in the past year? Do you have backup solutions (e.g., generator, mobile hotspot) to ensure your work is not affected?



(2) Home Setup: Will you be working from home? Is your workspace quiet and suitable for handling calls? Do you have your own computer or laptop? Please attach a screenshot of your current internet speed.



(3) Availability & Speed: Can you consistently work seven days a week, six hours per day, and remain available for the full shift without missed calls? How quickly do you typically answer inbound calls? What is your current typing speed in words per minute?



(4) Call & Complaint Experience: Describe your experience with answering inbound calls, booking or scheduling appointments, customer service, following scripts, and handling complaints. Provide one real example of how you de-escalated an upset client and the outcome.



(5) Tools & Systems: What programs, tools, phone systems, or VoIP platforms have you used for handling calls? Do you have any issues with background noise, power outages, or internet interruptions? How do you take notes and log client interactions during calls?



(6) Voice Recordings: Please submit two short voice recordings. Recording one should demonstrate booking an appointment from 7:00 PM to 8:00 PM with a total amount of $60. Recording two should demonstrate handling a double charge for a 90-minute massage where the client sees two $90 charges and is requesting a refund. Your tone should be calm, polite, and professional.



(7) Current Job: Do you currently have another job? If yes, where, how many hours per week, and what are your roles and tasks in that job?



(8) Schedule, Pay & Start Date: Are you able to work seven days a week, six hours per day, during Central Standard Time (CST) hours without conflicts? What is your expected hourly or monthly rate? What is your earliest physical start date if hired?



(9) Marketing, Design & Video: Do you have experience with online marketing, creating content using Canva, or producing short video clips such as
 Reels or similar platforms? Please describe your skills and provide samples of your previous work if available.



(10) Fit, Skills & Experience: Briefly introduce yourself and explain why you would be a good fit for this role. Include what you believe is most important when handling spa clients over the phone, especially during complaints, and what other skills or areas you have a lot of experience in.
